---
title: "Chapter 09: Recruitment and Retention of Study Participants"
description: "Detailed study notes for Chapter 9 of Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 9
source_pages: "249-267"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-recruitment-and-retention-of-study-participants:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-1:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Chapter 9</span><span>Source pages 249-267</span></div>

This note summarises Chapter 9, "Recruitment and Retention of Study Participants," from Kathryn E.
Newcomer, Harry P. Hatry, and Joseph S. Wholey's *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation*
[@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note
based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the chapter.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the chapter's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, checklists, figures, boxes, or extended passages.

## Core Argument

Recruitment and retention are design-quality issues because sample coverage and continued participation determine whether evaluation findings are credible.

## Study Summary

The chapter identifies common barriers to enrolling and keeping participants in evaluation studies.

It offers practical strategies for clearance, outreach, participant tracking, incentives, staff
roles, and realistic scheduling.

The main risk is underestimating the time, permissions, and relationship work needed to secure
usable data.

Within the wider book, this chapter belongs to Part 1: Evaluation Planning and Design. It should be
read alongside the neighboring chapters because the handbook treats design, data collection,
analysis, and use as connected evaluation work.

## Key Concepts

- **recruitment**: Processes for enrolling the right number and type of study participants.
- **retention**: Processes for keeping participants engaged and measured across the evaluation period.
- **evaluation design**: The plan linking questions, evidence, analysis, reporting, and intended use.

## Chapter Structure

- Design problem: Recruitment and retention are design-quality issues because sample coverage and continued participation determine whether evaluation findings are credible.
- Evidence focus: The chapter identifies common barriers to enrolling and keeping participants in evaluation studies.
- Practice method: It offers practical strategies for clearance, outreach, participant tracking, incentives, staff roles, and realistic scheduling.
- Quality risk: The main risk is underestimating the time, permissions, and relationship work needed to secure usable data.

## Practical Implications

- Plan recruitment and retention before data collection.
- Budget time for IRB and administrative clearance.
- Monitor enrollment and follow-up continuously.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Chapter 8: Conducting Case Studies](chapter-08-conducting-case-studies.qmd)
- Next: [Chapter 10: Designing, Managing, and Analyzing Multisite Evaluations](chapter-10-designing-managing-and-analyzing-multisite-evaluations.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Evaluation Planning and Design](section-01-planning-and-design.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations](section-02-planning-useful-evaluations.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation](section-03-performance-measurement.qmd)

## Study Prompts

- What decision or design problem does Chapter 9 help resolve?
- Which evidence source should be reopened before using this summary in applied work?
- Which quality risk in the chapter is most likely to affect a live evaluation?

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-recruitment-and-retention-of-study-participants:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-1:c0001`

## References
